response_constraints: Resolve the initial description once, then reuse the returned stable ID for both later updates.
Do not search again or switch entities after stage 1.
Treat the correction date as superseding the first requested date.
Never update either distractor record and never send a notification.
Report only facts established by tool results.
Present any multiple search candidates alphabetically by name.

stages.purpose: Resolve the user’s description to one stable ID before any mutation.
Reuse the ID established in stage 1 for the first requested date change.
Apply the correction to the same entity without searching again or touching distractors.

follow_up_turns.content: Now change the date on the resolved ID to 2026-11-10; no fresh search and no notification.
Correction: I gave you the wrong date. Set that same case to 2026-11-12 instead; this replaces 2026-11-10. Reuse the same ID, do not search again, and do not notify anyone.

prompt: Find the case named “Damaged parcel report” at Acme Co. and report its stable ID and current recorded date. This first turn is read-only; do not update it or notify anyone yet. If the search returns several candidates, present them alphabetically by name.
